task_yaml.task:
You are an office worker visiting Tokyo on a business trip for the first
time. Plan for next Saturday, when you only have 13:30-18:30 free, and
the fixed starting point is Tokyo Station Marunouchi North Exit. If next
Saturday is a public holiday, closed day, special-hours day, sold out, or
the official pages do not provide enough date-specific opening/admission
information, use the following Saturday instead and explain why. Use only
the browser and webpage information to create a half-day travel plan, and
save it to:

    /tmp_workspace/results/tokyo_saturday_afternoon_plan.md

The candidate attractions are limited to: Tokyo National Museum, Ueno Zoo,
teamLab Planets TOKYO, and Tokyo Skytree. You must first check the official
website for all four candidate attractions for opening hours, last admission
time, and reservation/ticket rules for the selected date, then use a map
webpage to check travel time from the starting point to attractions and
between attractions. The final plan must select exactly two attractions.

The final route must satisfy:
- Total sightseeing time plus transportation time is no more than 5 hours.
- Each individual transfer should preferably be no more than 35 minutes.
- Do not choose an attraction that requires complex same-day reservations
  when admission cannot be confirmed.
- Include one 15-30 minute coffee or rest break in the itinerary.

The Markdown must contain and be organized only around these five top-level
sections:
- chosen attractions
- official rule summary
- travel times
- final schedule
- why not choose the others

State the selected date in the final plan. Do not only provide links or list
attraction descriptions; provide an executable schedule and tradeoff
rationale.
